title: EEG and hedonic responses to sonic-seasoning soundscapes
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings and behavioral ratings from participants exposed to sonic-seasoning soundscapes. Each participant completed a session including eyes-open and eyes-closed baseline periods followed by approximately 17-second exposures to jungle and deconstruct soundscapes, with concurrent hedonic (liking) ratings. The dataset includes raw EEG data, event markers, participant demographics, baseline survey responses, and stimulus audio files, along with processed EEGLAB-compatible derivatives for secondary analysis.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using EPOC X hardware with bandpass filtering applied during acquisition. Each session consisted of resting-state calibration (eyes-open and eyes-closed baseline periods controlled by participant button press) followed by exposure to two soundscape conditions (jungle and deconstruct, ~17 s each). Participants provided liking ratings and completed baseline-state survey items. Recording timestamps were removed for de-identification; participant IDs are sequential and anonymized.
